art1.title: Xerez CD learn their rivals: the 17 teams in Group IV for 2026/27
art1.lead: The Spanish Football Federation confirms the make-up of the group for next season, featuring clubs from Andalusia, the Canary Islands and Extremadura. The official fixture list will be announced in the coming weeks.
body: The Royal Spanish Football Federation has finalised the structure of the Segunda Federación groups for the 2026/27 season. Xerez CD will once again compete in Group IV, alongside clubs from Andalusia, the Canary Islands and Extremadura.

Confirmed opponents include sides such as Atlético Sanluqueño, Marbella, Linares Deportivo, Recreativo de Huelva and Sevilla Atlético, as well as Canarian reserve teams Las Palmas Atlético, Tenerife B and Tamaraceite, plus Extremaduran clubs Badajoz and Don Benito.
The club approaches the new campaign aiming to fight for promotion to Primera Federación, after last season ended in the first round of the promotion play-offs against Conquense.
A detailed match-by-match calendar has yet to be confirmed; the Federation is expected to publish it in the coming weeks, with the regular season set to kick off in September.

art2.title: Xerez CD seal the signing of Rubén Mesa, Deportiva Minera's top scorer
art2.lead: The blue-and-white side strengthen their attack with a forward from Extremadura who scored thirteen goals last season between Estepona and Deportiva Minera.
body: Xerez CD have officially confirmed the arrival of Rubén Mesa for the 2026/27 season. The forward joins from Deportiva Minera, the Cartagena-based club with whom he played the second half of last season after leaving Estepona in the winter transfer window.

The Extremaduran striker ended last season as one of the most in-form forwards in Group IV: seven goals with Minera and six with Estepona, finishing as the Cartagena side's top scorer in the closing stretch of the campaign.
His arrival adds to the moves the sporting management has been closing this summer, as head coach Xavi Molist puts the finishing touches to his squad ahead of the league opener in September.

art3.title: "We're arriving hungry to fight for the top spots from day one", admits the captain
art3.lead: The team captain looks back on the start of pre-season, the arrival of new faces, and the goals for the campaign ahead.
body: Speaking informally after one of the first pre-season sessions, the Xerez captain expressed enthusiasm about the start of the new project: "You can feel a different atmosphere from day one, there's real eagerness to start competing as soon as possible."
On the summer signings, he highlighted the quality they bring to the dressing room: "The lads who have joined know the division well, and you can feel that in the day-to-day training."
Asked about the season's objective, he didn't hold back: "Last year we fell just short. This year we want to fight for the top from the very start."
art3.quote: "You can feel a different atmosphere from day one, there's real eagerness to start competing as soon as possible."

art4.title: Pre-season gets under way at Chapín with an eye on September
art4.lead: Almost the entire squad has begun training under Xavi Molist, with the league start date still to be confirmed.
body: Xerez CD have started the 2026/27 pre-season with most of the squad already available to the coaching staff. The opening sessions have focused on physical conditioning following the summer break.
Head coach Xavi Molist, who is taking charge of his first full season at the helm of the blue-and-whites, will have several weeks of work before the campaign's first official fixture.
The club has not yet finalised the pre-season friendly schedule, although the first warm-up opponents are expected to be confirmed in the coming days.

art5.title: Chapín unveils new floodlighting ahead of the new season
art5.lead: The municipal stadium renews its lighting system, improving visibility for both players and broadcasters.
body: The installation, jointly funded by the club and the local council, replaces the old floodlight system with state-of-the-art LED technology.
The upgrade arrives in time for the start of the new season, during which several matches could be played under lights for broadcasting reasons.
The club also points to the energy savings the renovation will bring in the medium term.

art6.title: Head coach reviews pre-season objectives ahead of the first friendly
art6.lead: Xavi Molist faced the media for the first time this summer, looking back on the opening weeks of work and previewing the keys to the season ahead.
body: "We're in a process of adaptation, both physically and tactically. We need time for the team to fully understand what we want", explained the Catalan coach in his first appearance of the summer.
On the transfer market, he was cautious: "We're still working with the club to finish putting the squad together. We want to reach September with a complete team."

art7.title: Supporters already preparing their backing for the league opener
art7.lead: Xerez fan groups announce their first initiatives to support the team from the very first match of the season.
body: Several Xerez supporter groups have begun coordinating to prepare a welcome for the team in the first official match of the season, once the league fixture list is confirmed.
The Chapín stands are once again expected to play an important role from the start of the competition, as has become customary in recent campaigns.

art8.title: This is how Segunda Federación Group IV looks for 2026/27
art8.lead: A look at the 18 teams that will make up the group this season, featuring sides from Andalusia, the Canary Islands and Extremadura.
body: Group IV of Segunda Federación will this season bring together historic Andalusian football clubs alongside reserve teams from La Liga and Segunda División, as well as representatives from the Canary Islands and Extremadura.

The competition format remains unchanged from previous seasons: a double round-robin league, with direct promotion for the champions and a promotion play-off for the clubs finishing between second and fifth.

art9.title: This is how the first week of pre-season is going at Chapín
art9.lead: Double training sessions, fitness tests and the first touches of the ball for an almost complete squad.
body: The first week of pre-season has focused on physical work, with double training sessions and the first fitness tests for the entire squad.
The coaching staff have gradually introduced ball work into training, ahead of bringing in the first tactical concepts in the coming weeks.

art10.title: These are the players the head coach has available to start pre-season
art10.lead: A look at the initial core of the squad, while the last incoming signings of the summer are finalised.
body: With only a handful of players under contract before the start of the summer, the club has worked at a good pace to confirm both renewals and new arrivals.
Alongside the names already confirmed, several other players remain in negotiation, in a transfer market that is expected to keep moving in the coming weeks.

art11.title: Club sets date for official squad presentation at Chapín
art11.lead: Supporters will get their first look at the complete squad ahead of the first pre-season friendly.
body: Xerez CD are preparing the official presentation event for the 2026/27 squad, where fans will see all the summer's new faces alongside the new coaching staff for the first time.
The club has not yet confirmed the exact date, though the event is expected to take place in the coming weeks, ahead of the first pre-season friendly.

art12.title: Fitness tests and physical work mark the first days of pre-season
art12.lead: The coaching staff prioritise physical conditioning before introducing the first tactical concepts.
body: The first days of pre-season have been marked by fitness tests and base physical work, typical of this stage of the calendar.
The coaching staff's goal is to arrive in the best possible physical condition for the first pre-season friendly, still without a confirmed date.

art13.title: Club continues working the transfer market ahead of the league start
art13.lead: The sporting management has not ruled out further incomings before the summer transfer window closes.
body: In addition to the moves already confirmed, Xerez CD continue working the transfer market with the aim of having the squad finalised before the start of the official competition.
The club is urging caution until each deal is fully completed, while acknowledging the market is expected to keep moving in the coming weeks.

art14.title: Zelu and Chuma set to stay for another season as Xerez players
art14.lead: Xerez CD continue shaping their squad for 2026/27 with the continuity of two familiar faces in the division: winger Zelu and forward Chuma.
body: Xerez CD continue putting together the squad that will play under Xavi Molist in the 2026/27 season. In addition to the already confirmed renewal of Guille Campos, the club is close to finalising the continuity of both Zelu and Chuma, who will remain for another season as Xerez players.
Zelu, one of the few players who already had a contract in place at Xerez CD, thus extends his stay with the blue-and-white side, having become a regular presence in attack over recent seasons.

Meanwhile, Rafael González Rodríguez, 'Chuma', a Seville native, will embark on his second consecutive season as a blue-and-white after returning to the club last January, in his second spell as a Xerez player, arriving from Poland's K.S. Wieczysta Kraków. Coming through the Sevilla FC academy, the forward has built a wide-ranging career with clubs including UB Lebrijana, Córdoba CF, San Roque de Lepe, UCAM Murcia and Mérida AD.
Both renewals add to the moves already confirmed this summer, in a transfer window in which the club is also working to officially confirm the arrival of Fali Giménez in the coming days.

art15.title: Xerez CD bolster their goalkeeping with the arrival of Carlos Pantoja
art15.lead: The Madrid-born goalkeeper signs for the 2026/27 season after a campaign of complete trust at RSD Alcalá in Segunda Federación.
body: Xerez CD have officially confirmed the signing of Carlos Pantoja Valadés, a goalkeeper born in Pozuelo de Alarcón (Madrid) in 2000, who arrives to take over between the posts following the departure of Ángel de la Calzada, last season's Group Zamora Trophy winner.

Pantoja arrives with a solid track record: he started all 34 league matches for RSD Alcalá, playing over 3,000 minutes and conceding only 39 goals, a tally that made his side one of the league's least breached defences. Before his spell at Alcalá he played for clubs such as Rayo Vallecano B, Complutense Alcalá and CF Collado Villalba, gaining experience in both Segunda and Tercera Federación.

The club highlights his command of his area, his strength in the air and his leadership from the back, qualities that head coach Xavi Molist's staff consider key to the project for next season in Group IV.

art16.title: Manu Galán, the summer's first signing, arrives to strengthen the back line
art16.lead: The Sevillian centre-back joins from Deportiva Minera, the Murcian club that fell just short of promotion to Primera Federación.
body: Manuel Galán Ubero, known as 'Manu Galán', a Seville native, becomes Xerez CD's first confirmed signing for the 2026/27 season. The centre-back arrives after a season with Deportiva Minera, the Murcian club that came close to direct promotion in Group IV.

Coming through the Sevilla FC academy, Galán has played for a number of clubs on his way to Jerez: Utrera, Cabecense, Azuaga and Huesca B in Tercera, before joining Recreativo de Huelva, with whom he achieved two consecutive promotions, and a spell at Melilla before his most recent stint at Minera.
The club is relying on his experience and leadership qualities to add solidity to a defence that head coach Xavi Molist is putting the finishing touches to ahead of the league opener in September.

art17.title: Guille Campos extends his stay at Xerez CD until 2027
art17.lead: The defender from Sanlúcar de Barrameda continues for another season after being a key figure in the division's best defensive record.
body: Xerez CD have confirmed the renewal of Guille Campos until the end of the 2026/27 season. The Sanlúcar-born defender played 31 matches last season, 30 of them as a starter, racking up over 2,500 minutes and one goal.
His consistency and versatility to play in different positions across the back line were, according to the club, key factors in Xerez CD finishing last season as the league's least-breached side.
With this renewal, the coaching staff retain one of the most stable pieces of the defensive block ahead of the new project led by Xavi Molist.

art18.title: Zequi Díaz to continue for another season in the blue-and-white attack
art18.lead: The winger from Sanlúcar de Barrameda, who joined as a winter reinforcement last season, remains under contract for 2026/27.
body: Ezequiel Díaz Romero, known as 'Zequi Díaz', will remain a Xerez CD player for another season. The right-footed winger, who joined the club in last season's winter transfer window, played 16 matches, 12 of them as a starter, scoring two goals.
The club particularly values his dribbling ability, individual skill and the danger he creates from the flank, qualities the coaching staff want to keep making the most of in attack next season.
His continued stay adds to that of other players already confirmed, in a squad Xerez CD continue to shape ahead of the start of the competition.

art19.title: Ismael Gutiérrez stays for another year in the Xerez midfield
art19.lead: The midfielder, last season's MVP, extends his stay with the club after being a key player for the team last season.
body: Xerez CD have confirmed the continuity of Ismael Gutiérrez Montilla, who will play his second season in Jerez after a campaign in which he was a key player for the team, scoring two goals.
The midfielder ended last season as the 2025/26 MVP, a recognition the club attributes to his composure on the ball, his ability to balance the team, and the leadership he showed in midfield.
His renewal adds continuity to one of the key positions in Xavi Molist's system, who already counts on one of his most decisive players for the 2026/27 project.

art20.title: Fali close to becoming a Xerez CD player after months training at Chapín
art20.lead: The Valencian centre-back, formerly of Cádiz CF, has been training with the blue-and-whites since February and is reportedly close to signing as a new addition to the back line.
body: Fali Giménez, a 32-year-old defender from Valencia, is reportedly very close to becoming a new Xerez CD player for the 2026/27 season, according to several local media outlets. The club has not yet confirmed the move officially.

The centre-back began training with the blue-and-white side last February, after leaving Cádiz CF, while deciding his professional future. Coming through the Villarreal and Levante academies, he has eight seasons as a Cádiz player under his belt, four of them in Primera División, as well as spells at Huracán de Valencia, Nástic de Tarragona and Barcelona B.

His profile, capable of playing both as a centre-back and a defensive midfielder, would make for a reliable addition to a back line head coach Xavi Molist is still putting together ahead of the start of the competition.

art21.title: Josete extends his stay at Xerez CD for the 2026/27 season
art21.lead: The centre-back from Elche will play his third consecutive season as a blue-and-white, having been an important player in the Xerez back line and a mainstay of the dressing room.
body: Xerez CD have confirmed the continuity of José Antonio Malagón Rubio, 'Josete', a centre-back born in Elche in 1988, who will continue wearing the blue-and-white shirt during the 2026/27 season, his third consecutive campaign with the Jerez-based club.

The experienced defender has been an important player in the Xerez back line throughout last season, bringing toughness, aerial ability and the capacity to organise the team from the back. Beyond what he offers on the pitch, Josete has also become a mainstay of the dressing room, thanks to his experience and his influence on the team's day-to-day life.
His renewal gives continuity to one of the most valued pieces of the blue-and-white project, both on and off the pitch, ahead of the 2026/27 season.

art23.title: Xerez CD considering paying the release clause of Javi Cheli, young San Roque de Lepe midfielder
art23.lead: According to sources close to the club, the blue-and-white sporting management are exploring a move for the sub-23 midfielder, one of last season's breakout performers in Tercera RFEF.
body: Xerez Club Deportivo are reportedly considering paying the release clause of Javi Cheli, a young midfielder at CD San Roque de Lepe, according to sources close to the club. If confirmed, the move would not yet have official backing from either party.

Cheli, a sub-23 player and son of the former Recreativo de Huelva player of the same nickname, had a notable season in Tercera RFEF with the Lepe-based club, where he remains under contract until 2027. His strong form, marked by his ability to arrive from midfield and his composure on the ball, has attracted interest from several higher-division clubs.

San Roque de Lepe have already publicly acknowledged that the midfielder's future is not settled, and that he could leave if a club is willing to pay the clause set out in his contract. Among the clubs reportedly interested in the player, according to reports in Huelva-based media, are Recreativo de Huelva, Cádiz CF and even RCD Mallorca.

Should the move go through, Cheli's arrival would represent a bet on young local talent, in a season in which Xerez CD continue working to strengthen their midfield ahead of the start of the competition.

art24.title: Xerez CD confirm start of training and their first friendly match
art24.lead: The club has announced that the 2026/27 pre-season will begin on 20 July, with the first warm-up match set for 1 August against Jerez Industrial.
body: Xerez CD have confirmed the first dates of the 2026/27 pre-season. The squad will begin training on 20 July, kicking off physical and technical preparation ahead of the new campaign.
The first friendly of the summer will come on 1 August, against Jerez Industrial, a fixture that has become a regular pre-season opener in recent years.

The club has not yet confirmed the rest of the friendly schedule, though further pre-season opponents are expected to be announced in the coming weeks ahead of the start of the official competition in September.

art25.title: Xerez CD open the league on 6 September against Estepona and close at Badajoz on 9 May
art25.lead: The RFEF has released the full Group IV Segunda Federación fixture list. Xerez CD begin at home against CD Estepona and wrap up the regular phase away at Badajoz.
body: Xerez CD now know their full roadmap for the 2026/27 season. The Royal Spanish Football Federation has published the complete fixture list for Group IV of Segunda Federación, which kicks off on 6 September with Xerez CD hosting CD Estepona at the Estadio Municipal de Chapín.

The regular phase will conclude on 9 May, with Xerez CD making the trip to Badajoz — one of the toughest venues in the group. In between, 34 rounds of home and away fixtures lay out a long and demanding competition against sides from Andalusia, Extremadura and the Canary Islands.

Xerez CD go into the season with an openly stated aim of challenging for promotion to Primera Federación. The opener against Estepona at Chapín will be an early indicator of the level of the squad that the coaching staff have been building throughout pre-season.

art27.title: Xerez CD in contact with David García, ex-Cádiz midfielder who spent last season on loan at Ibiza
art27.lead: The Jerez club have opened talks with the midfielder, who spent last season on loan at UD Ibiza from Cádiz CF.
body: Xerez CD have made contact with David García, a midfielder who arrived at Cádiz CF and spent last season on loan at UD Ibiza, this newspaper can confirm. The player is now a free agent following the end of his contract with the Cádiz club, and is seen as a realistic option to strengthen the Xerez midfield.

García is a midfielder with experience at professional and semi-professional level, comfortable combining in tight spaces and with the engine to cover ground. His profile would suit the system Xavi Molist wants to implement for the coming season.
Talks are at an early stage and no agreement has been reached yet. The sporting department continues to work on finalising the squad ahead of the start of pre-season on 20 July.

art26.title: Xerez CD confirm the signing of Fali, the former Cádiz man who has been training at Chapín for months
art26.lead: Fali Giménez puts pen to paper with Xerez CD, becoming the club's fourth summer signing. The 32-year-old Valencia-born centre-back had been training with the squad since February following his departure from Cádiz CF.
body: Xerez CD have confirmed the signing of Fali Giménez for the 2026/27 season. The Valencia-born centre-back, who had been training alongside the rest of the squad at Chapín for several months following his exit from Cádiz CF, is now officially a Xerez player.

Fali, 32, brings a wealth of professional experience. During his time at Cádiz he spent eight seasons at the club, four of them in La Liga. He came through the academies at Villarreal and Levante, and has also played for Nàstic de Tarragona and Barcelona B. His versatility is an added bonus: a specialist centre-back, he can also operate as a defensive midfielder.

His arrival, which had been widely expected despite his name being linked with CD Eldense in recent weeks, significantly strengthens the defensive unit of Xavi Molist's side. Xerez CD now have four summer signings to their name, following Manu Galán, Carlos Pantoja and Rubén Mesa.
